final_response: Logged. Added the recurring contractor-access provisioning lag (Feb / Mar / May 2026, ~4 days each) to observations.md as a pattern, opened a followup to decide whether to raise it with IT or propose a pre-provisioning workflow, and noted today's incident in the daybook.

actions.why: Orient to workspace conventions before routing.
Check existing state of observations.md, followups.md, decisions.md, daybook/, projects/ — all empty / unpopulated.
The operator flagged a recurring cross-session pattern (3 instances over 3+ months). Observations.md is the right home per AGENTS.md ('descriptive cross-session notes worth remembering').
The operator's framing ('so we don't lose track') implies an outstanding action — decide what to do about the pattern. Routed as a followup.
Capture today's incident in the daily log with pointers to the observation and followup entries.

notes: Operator's signal had two layers: a single incident (today's 4-day lag) and a pattern claim (also happened in Feb and Mar). The pattern is the load-bearing content — a single incident would be a daybook entry only.
Did not open a project folder. There's no scoped project yet; this is a cross-cutting operational pattern. If it becomes a named initiative (e.g., 'contractor-onboarding-fix'), promote then.
Did not write a decision record. No decision has been made yet — only a pattern noted and a followup to decide. decisions.md stays clean until an actual call is made.
Did not escalate or contact IT. Out of scope for a flat-file workspace agent; the followup leaves that judgment to the operator.
